2001-06-01.title: Indonesian Fires 4 Ministers in Frantic Bid to Stay in Office
2001-06-01.abstract: JAKARTA, Indonesia, June 1 — Maneuvering desperately to cling to power, President Abdurrahman Wahid dismissed four cabinet ministers today, including the security minister and the national police chief, who had opposed Mr. Wahid's threats to declare a state of emergency.

2001-06-01.title: World Briefing | Europe: Czech Republic: Leak At Nuclear Reactor
2001-06-01.abstract: Radioactive water leaks from nuclear reactor during tests at power plant in Temelin in Czech Republic; mishap renews tensions with Austria, just 35 miles away; map (S)

2001-06-01.title: World Briefing | Americas: Canada: Saudi Prince Cancels Visit
2001-06-01.abstract: Saudi Crown Prince Abdullah cancels visit to Canada following reports that William Sampson, Canadian held in Saudi jail, has been tortured; Sampson was arrested in connection with bombings last year, and Saudi government complains of 'meddling' in case; Abdullah photo (S)

2001-06-01.title: A Palestinian Champion Of Living in Peace Is Dead
2001-06-01.abstract: Faisal Husseini, senior Palestinian official in Jerusalem, dies unexpectedly of heart attack at age of 60, prompting outpouring of grief from Palestinians and from Israeli doves who considered him man of integrity and vital interlocutor; Husseini dies in Kuwait, while attending conference on Israel; he had been mentioned as possible successor to Yasir Arafat; photo (M)

2001-06-01.title: U.N. Assembly Votes to Condemn Attacks on Religious Sites
2001-06-01.abstract: General Assembly votes to condemn all attacks on religious sites, and calls on governments and international organizations to take responsibility for protection of religious and cultural treasures; resolution follows ransacking of mosques and churches in Balkans and destruction of giant Buddhas in Afghanistan (S)

2001-06-02.title: An Upriver Battle for Votes in Peru
2001-06-02.abstract: Alejandro Toledo pursues presidency of Peru with campaign that is pure spectacle, rich in personal scandal that obscures his message and damages his credibility; Toledo denies allegations ranging from sex with prostitutes and cocaine use to domestic violence and money laundering, but he barely won third of vote in first round of balloting and is struggling to maintain shaky lead over Alan Garcia in runoff; photo; map (M)

2001-06-02.title: 16 KILLED BY SUICIDE BOMBER OUTSIDE TEL AVIV NIGHTCLUB
2001-06-02.abstract: Suicide bomber sets off powerful explosion outside crowded beachside club in Tel Aviv, killing least 16 people, mostly young Israelis, in deadliest terror attack since eruption of Palestinian violence last fall; photo; Prime Min Ariel Sharon convenes cabinet meeting that is expected to end unilateral cease-fire; government puts blame on Yasir Arafat; Palestinian Authority condemns bombing, but calls for restraint; bomber apparently mingled with young crowd before setting off bomb packed with bullets and nails; Pres Bush calls on Arafat to act on cease-fire (M)

2001-06-02.title: U.N. Names Special AIDS Envoy for Africa
2001-06-02.abstract: UN names Canadian diplomat, Stephen Lewis, as special envoy for AIDS in Africa (S)

2001-06-02.title: U.N. Chief Calls on U.S. Companies to Donate to AIDS Fund
2001-06-02.abstract: UN Sec Gen Kofi Annan appeals to US corporations to give to planned multibillion-dollar global fund to fight AIDS; meets with Sec Colin Powell, who has joined Annan in high-profile, personal campaign to spotlight Africa's AIDS tragedy (M)

2001-06-02.title: Royal Family of Nepal Is Shot Dead in Palace
2001-06-02.abstract: Nepal's Crown Prince Dipendra shoots his parents, King Birenda and Queen Aiswarya, and several other relatives to death in palace in Katmandu, and then kills himself; photos; also slain are 30-year-old prince's brother Nirajan and sister Shruti; Nepalese official says prince entered sitting room, dressed in army fatigues and opened fire with automatic rifle; says as many as 11 people were killed; BBC reports Diprenda had quarreled with mother over his choice of bride; privy council is expected to choose successor, probably king's brother, Gyanendra, who was out of capital at time of slayings; survivors reportedly include Prince Dhirendra (M)

2001-06-03.title: China's Inner Circle Reveals Big Unrest, and Lists Causes
2001-06-03.abstract: Communist Party's inner sanctum issues startlingly frank report describing spreading pattern of 'collective protests and group incidents' arising from economic, ethnic and religious conflicts in China and saying that relations between party officials and masses are 'tense, with conflicts on the rise'; report, published by Central Committee press, describes mounting public anger over inequality, corruption and official aloofness and paints picture of seething unrest; warns that coming years of rapid change--driven in part by China's plans to accelerate opening of its markets to foreign trade and investment--are likely to mean even greater social conflict; it is unclear why central party officials have broken with tradition of suppressing such sensitive information (M)

2001-06-03.title: Protests Rise in China
2001-06-03.abstract: An unusual report from the Communist Party describes a wave of protests and says they may get worse as the nation opens up more.    Article, Page 14.

2001-06-03.title: Saudis Pick Exxon Mobil and Shell for Huge Gas Projects
2001-06-03.abstract: Exxon Mobil Corp, world's largest publicly traded oil company, is chosen to lead $17 billion gas development project in Al Ghawar field in Saudi Arabia, world's largest oil field; Royal Dutch/Shell Group will head $4 billion development of Shaybah, also in Saudi Arabia; Foreign Min Saud al-Faisal says two companies were chosen for lead roles because of their expertise (S)

2001-06-03.title: Victims' Accounts of a Night of Horror
2001-06-03.abstract: Teenage survivors of suicide bombing at Tel Aviv nightclub recall horror of event; tell of chaos after the explosion, of seeing people fall in tangled heap as wounded tried to drag themselves to safety; speak of their grief at learning that friends died in blast; near site of bombing, hundreds of outraged Israelis call for war against Palestinians (M)

2001-06-03.title: British Dislike Of the Euro Is Eroding In Campaign
2001-06-03.abstract: Britain must decide whether to join 12 European nations that are swapping their national currencies for euro in January; it is most important single decison country will make in immediate future; debate has tapped into broad British cultural reluctance to embrace notions and customs of Continental origin, as well as specific fears of consequences to highly performing British economy of submitting it to interest rates and tax decisions made in Brussels rather than London; three weeks ago, 70 percent of Britons polled opposed exchanging pound for euro; that has changed in past weeks, as euro's fiercest opponent, William Hague, succeeded in turning election into contest over Britain's relationship with rest of Europe and his strategy backfired (M)

2001-06-04.title: An Icon's Birthday Has Indonesia Looking to Past
2001-06-04.abstract: Indonesians look to past after months of political intrigue involving Pres Abdurrahman Wahid, who faces possible impeachment; are preparing celebrations of 100th anniversary of birth of Sukarno, nation's founding president, whose rehabilitation has been under way since successor, Suharto, was toppled in 1998 has gathered force in current crisis (M)

2001-06-04.title: RUMSFELD LIMITING MILITARY CONTACTS WITH THE CHINESE
2001-06-04.abstract: Defense Sec Donald Rumsfeld cuts virtually all Pentagon contacts with Chinese armed forces in move prompting concern among China experts within US military establishment; is personally deciding which contacts should be allowed and has rejected overwhelming majority, despite Pentagon's claims that its is conducting case-by-case review and that no sweeping decisions have been made; no direct contact between American and Chinese military officers has been authorized in recent months; senior Defense Dept aides say decisions are aimed at signaling deep displeasure over China's handling of collision between Chinese fighter and US Navy reconnaissance plane over South China Sea, which resulted in 11-day detention of crew, loss of Chinese pilot and weeks of wrangling over return of aircraft; Bush administration had taken more skeptical approach to China, even before collision, but had maintained military-to-military ties (M)

2001-06-04.title: Youths Rally for Re-election of Iran's Reformist President
2001-06-04.abstract: High school students stage large rally in Tehran for Iran's reformist president, Mohammad Khatami, who is seeking re-election on June 8; he faces daunting obstacles, notably crackdown by hard-line Islamic clerics, who hold real power (M)

2001-06-04.title: Immigrants Bury Their Friends, but the Hopes Live On
2001-06-04.abstract: Israel buries victims of suicide bombing in Tel Aviv discotheque in daylong procession of grief; many among 19 killed were Russian-speaking immigrants; photo (M)

2001-06-04.title: Wells Klein, 74; Worked to Help Refugees
2001-06-04.abstract: Wells C Klein, longtime advocate for rights of immigrants and refugees wanting to settle in United States, dies at age 74; photo (S)

2001-06-05.title: Blair's Laborites: Not Lovable, but Electable
2001-06-05.abstract: Public opinion polls show Britain's Labor Party, led by Prime Min Tony Blair, heads into June 7 national elections 17 points ahead of Tory opposition; party could increase current edge in 650-seat House of Commons by 10 percent, to about 200 seats; despite apparent popularity, party which assumed power in 1997 on wave of euphoria accompanying end of 18 years of Tory rule, is generally perceived to have failed to live up to expectations; has developed reputation for being arrogant, overly enthusiastic about its power, ruthless about stifling dissent--particularly in Parliament--and obsessed with buffing its image; is perceived as party of control freaks, reputation enhanced by its tight rein on press; despite its failures and shortcomings, many Britons--even those disillusioned with Labor--feel Tory opposition is far worse; photo (M)

2001-06-05.title: Revisiting Tiananmen: Envoy Saw U.S. Error in 'Cozying Up'
2001-06-05.abstract: National Security Archive, private research firm, releases CIA and State Department documents on 12th anniversary of Tiananmen Square massacre in Beijing, China, showing US Amb James Lilley in June 1989 cabled Sec of State James A Baker 3rd suggesting that Pres Bush may have badly miscalculated policy toward Beijing; he cited decision to send American naval vessels on official visit to Shanghai in May, which he said created impression that Washington was 'cozying up' to Chinese military just as protests were erupting (M)

2001-06-05.title: Riots Break Out as Nepal Gets 3rd King in 4 Days
2001-06-05.abstract: Rioting erupts in Katmandu, Nepal, hours after enthronement of King Gyanendra, third monarch in four days; government imposes curfew; protestors hurl rocks and accusations and demand truth about slaying of nearly entire royal family; enthronement follows death from gunshot wounds of King Dipendra, son of King Birendra and Queen Aiswarya, who is suspected of slaying them along with brothers, sisters and uncle in royal palace before turning weapon on himself in reported disagreement with parents over his choice of bride; some protestors openly blame Gyanendra, who described killings as accident--explanation that is considered preposterous by many; he has promised full public disclosure following investigation; photos (M)

2001-06-05.title: World Briefing | Americas: Mexico: Curbing Greenhouse Gases
2001-06-05.abstract: Pemex, Mexico's state-run energy syndicate and world's fifth-largest oil company, will begin voluntary program to limit its carbon dioxide emissions; it is first Latin American company to pledge to reduce greenhouse gases (S)

2001-06-05.title: Monarchy Is Still at the Heart of Many Nations in Asia
2001-06-05.abstract: Reverence for monarchy has remained strong in Asia, in places like Nepal, despite modernizing trends that have transformed many countries in region economically, socially and politically (S)

2001-06-06.title: Hague Manages to Keep a Stiff Upper Lip : The Designated Loser Slogs On in U.K. Race
2001-06-06.abstract: NEWARK, England—  Third in a series of articles

2001-06-06.title: West's Slump and Intifada Hit Israel's Pocketbook
2001-06-06.abstract: Israeli economy, booming until last fall, slows to crawl, battered by eight months of Palestinian violence and bludgeoned by Nasdaq; at least 25 hotels have closed, white-hot real estate market in high-tech hinterlands of Tel Aviv has cooled and Jerusalem apartments in range of guns are vacant; bank and airline losses, rising unemployment and falling exports and foreign investments also noted; graphs (M)

2001-06-06.title: World Briefing | Asia: India: Military Deal With Russia
2001-06-06.abstract: India and Russia agree to develop military transport plane and new generation of jet fighters for India (S)

2001-06-06.title: World Briefing | Europe: Greece: Immigrants Seek Legal Status
2001-06-06.abstract: Thousands of illegal immigrants flood government offices in Athens and other cities seeking to avert deportation by providing evidence that they have lived in Greece for at least one year; government suggests that Greece is home to about 800,000 illegal aliens, mostly Albanians (S)

2001-06-06.title: Navy Officer Missing in Philippines After Ambush by Guerrillas
2001-06-06.abstract: US Navy lieutenant Scott Alan Washburn is missing on Mount Pinatubo in Philippines after becoming separated from small group of US service personnel that came under fire from guerrillas; Washburn is believed to have plunged into jungle for safety after hearing gunfire (S)

2001-06-07.title: World Briefing | Americas: Argentina: Arrest In Arms Case
2001-06-07.abstract: Martin Balza, retired Argentine general who served as former Pres Carlos Saul Menem's army chief, is arrested on charges of taking part in plot to smuggle arms to Croatia and Ecuador in 1995 (S)

2001-06-07.title: World Briefing | Americas: Peru: Economist To Join Cabinet
2001-06-07.abstract: Peru's Pres-elect Alejandro Toledo will appoint Pedro-Pablo Kuczynski, orthodox economist popular on Wall Street, to cabinet (S)

2001-06-07.title: Rumsfeld Says U.S. Must Be in Balkans, but Not Become Mired
2001-06-07.abstract: Defense Sec Donald H Rumsfeld says American troops are still needed in Kosovo to stem violence, but warns that United States should proceed cautiously in Balkans and elsewhere to avoid becoming mired in extended conflicts where American interests are unclear; also says US should be wary of tendency of some missions to grow beyond their original scope and focus; Rumsfeld has made no secret of his desire to scale back American peacekeeping missions in certain parts of world, particularly Bosnia, where 3,500 American troops are stationed (M)

2001-06-07.title: World Briefing | Middle East: Jordan: Sanctions-Busting Air Service
2001-06-07.abstract: Royal Jordanian Airlines, in defiance of UN sanctions, resumes regular passenger service between Amman and Baghdad; map (S)

2001-06-08.title: BLAIR LEADS LABOR TO SECOND SWEEP OVER THE TORIES
2001-06-08.abstract: Prime Min Tony Blair and Labor Party sweep to victory in Britain's election, sending Conservatives to their second consecutive large-scale defeat and guaranteeing Labor its first re-election to a full term in office; William Hague, Conservative leader, concedes defeat; Labor is winning with majority similar to landslide 179-seat margin it received when it came to power in May 1997; photos (L)

2001-06-08.title: Russia's New Reach: Gas Pipeline to Turkey
2001-06-08.abstract: Russia is building pipeline that will bring natural gas from Russia, under the Black Sea, to energy-hungry Turkey; $3 billion project is daring engineering feat, in which pipeline will be laid at record depths of 7,000 feet in corrosive mud at bottom of sea, and its success would represent setback in American efforts to curtail Russia's influence in one of world's most strategic regions; pipeline would increase Turkey's dependence on Russian natural gas from current 66 percent to around 80 percent; American diplomats from the outset have warned that project would give Russia too much leverage over Turkey, and that it would constrain gas development in neighboring countries; photo; map (L)

2001-06-08.title: Tory Chief Steps Down After Labor's 2nd Election Victory
2001-06-08.abstract: LONDON, June 8 — William Hague announced today that he was stepping down as Conservative Party leader after returns from Thursday's election showed that the party had suffered its second straight devastating defeat. Filling the new opening at the top is likely to plunge the quarrelsome Tories into fresh dispute.

2001-06-08.title: A Witness To Massacre In Nepal Tells Gory Details
2001-06-08.abstract: Dr Rajiv Shahi, son-in-law of youngest brother of slain Nepalese King Birendra, and a survivor of palace massacre, gives first eyewitness account of royal blood bath; describes how Crown Prince Dipendra, falling-down drunk, nevertheless managed to navigate his way around dining hall, shooting family members as he tottered; most people in Katmandu remain skeptical; photos (M)

2001-06-08.title: Rumsfeld Outlines to NATO Fast Track for Missile Shield
2001-06-08.abstract: Defense Sec Donald H Rumsfeld tells NATO counterparts that United States is likely to deploy certain antiballistic missile systems before testing on them is completed, signaling speed with which Bush administration hopes to develop and use still-unproven technology; outlines two-tiered approach in which administration intends to continue consultations with its skeptical allies and Russia, even as Pentagon moves as swiftly as possible to develop and deploy systems (M)

2001-06-09.title: World Briefing | United Nations: Entire Council To Visit Kosovo
2001-06-09.abstract: All 15 Security Council members plan to travel to Kosovo to observe situation as United Nations administration in territory prepares for fall election there, first step to autonomy (S)

2001-06-09.title: Judge Nullifies Election of Ex-Minister In Malaysia
2001-06-09.abstract: KUALA LUMPUR—  A senior judge on Friday nullified an election victory of a member of Malaysia's governing party and criticized an apparent attempt to influence his decision, calling it an "insult to one's intelligence."

2001-06-09.title: Macedonia Expands Attacks Against Rebels
2001-06-09.abstract: Macedonian government intensifies its attacks against ethnic Albanian rebels, despite rebel offer of cease-fire; Prime Min Ljubco Georgievski, under intense international and local pressure, withdraws his call for declaration of state of war; Pres Boris Trajkovski says he is preparing peace plan to present to Parliament that would offer partial amnesty to rebel fighters; Macedonian Slav members of government are at odds over how to deal with growing insurgency, with Georgievski wanting to crush rebels by military means and others heeding Western admonitions to offer political concessions to persuade rebels to disarm and withdraw; photos (M)

2001-06-09.title: World Briefing | Africa: Western Sahara: Refugees Need Food
2001-06-09.abstract: Two United Nations agencies appeal for donations of $1.2 million a month to feed 155,000 refugees from disputed territory between Morocco and Algeria known as Western Sahara (S)

2001-06-10.title: Iran's Volatile Experiment: Voters Elect to Proceed
2001-06-10.abstract: Analysis of Pres Mohammad Khatami's landslide re-election victory as sign vast majority of Iran's electorate believes it still has both stake in electoral system and some hope for reform; notes those who advocated voting prevailed over boycott proponents (M)s

2001-06-10.title: Shaken Japan Tries to Face Aftermath of School Attack
2001-06-10.abstract: Families prepare to bury eight children slain at Ikeda, Japan, elementary school as Japan struggles to come to terms with mass slaying that shattered its image as safe, orderly haven; area residents bring flowers and incense sticks to school's gate; school says counselors will begin working with students, many of whom witnessed attack by man with knife, in which at least 15 other people were hurt (M)

2001-06-10.title: Fatherly Advice To the President On North Korea
2001-06-10.abstract: Former Pres George Bush reportedly sent Pres Bush, his son, memo by Donald P Gregg arguing need to reopen talks with North Korea before administration announced plan to seek talks; wanted son to adopt more moderate stand than that backed by Pentagon; memo is first concrete evidence of elder Bush's hand in specific policy arena; Bush spokesman says memo was sent to Condoleezza Rice, national security adviser; says administration highly respects Gregg (M)

2001-06-10.title: Ulster Voters Hand Victory To Opponents Of Peace Pact
2001-06-10.abstract: Results of Parliamentary elections in Northern Ireland casts future of fragile peace accord in doubt; Protestant opponents of agreement, led by Rev Ian Paisley, gain two new seats, while pro-agreement forces led by David Trimble loses three of nine; voting in Catholic areas shows Sinn Fein, political wing of Irish Republican Army, taking power away from moderate Social and Democratic Labor Party, increasing number of seats from two to four, while competitor remains with three (M)

2001-06-10.title: Iran Reform Mandate
2001-06-10.abstract: A huge turnout of voters in Iran gave President Mohammad Khatami the thumping victory his supporters say he needs to revive his drive for greater democracy and social freedom.

2001-06-11.title: Switzerland Narrowly Votes To Arm Troops
2001-06-11.abstract: Swiss voters, by 51 to 49 percent, approve arming soldiers taking part in international peacekeeping missions and allowing their citizen army to train with NATO forces (S)

2001-06-11.title: Dark Side of Security Quest: Squalor on an Atoll
2001-06-11.abstract: High-tech US Army base on Kawjalein Atoll in Marshall Islands has remained since 1950's center of US attempt to design missiles and missile defenses, but is also closest thing US has to colonial relationship; residents of 29-atoll archipelago, displaced from homes since atomic tests on Bikini Atoll, feel hopeless about link with Americans, who won control of 1,225 low-lying coral islands from Japan in 1944; Kwajalein, 1.2-square-mile island with idyllic, small-town America feel, is segregated from squalor of local Marshall Islanders on other atolls and is sometimes referred to as 'the country club;' maps; photo (M)

2001-06-11.title: U.S. Drug Indictment Chronicles King of Cancún's Fall
2001-06-11.abstract: Mario Villaneuva, powerful governor of Mexican state of Quintana Roo from 1993 to 1999, may become highest-ranking Latin American politician to face cocaine charges in US Court since arrest of Gen Manuel Noriega, Panamanian dictator, in 1989; is accused of helping 200 tons of cocaine into U; is in prison awaiting extradition; denies allegations; charges against him discussed; photos (M)

2001-06-11.title: Conservatives In Iran Face Reassessment Of Reforms
2001-06-11.abstract: Supporters of Pres Mohammad Khatami's attempts to create freer, more democratic Islamic republic in Iran say his overwhelming re-election victory should prompt his conservative opponents to reassess their attempts to frustrate change; Khatami won 21.7 million of 28.2 million votes cast (M)

2001-06-11.title: U.S. Cutbacks in Yemen
2001-06-11.abstract: State Department reduces personnel at its embassy in Sana, Yemen, and suspends services to public, citing increased threat since October 2000 attack on United States Navy vessel in Aden, country's main port (S)

2001-06-12.title: U.N. Review Criticizes Director Of Its Drug and Crime Program
2001-06-12.abstract: Investigations of UN Office of Drug Control and Crime Prevention in Vienna finds poor management is costing it money and talent, leaving staff demoralized and often in dark about decisions made at top (S)

2001-06-12.title: Suharto in Hospital After Collapsing
2001-06-12.abstract: BANGKOK, Thailand, June 12 — Former President Suharto of Indonesia was taken to a hospital in Jakarta this afternoon after he collapsed at his home from what a doctor called dangerously low blood pressure and a weak heart.

2001-06-12.title: World Briefing | United Nations: Stop The Music, Envoy Says
2001-06-12.abstract: Tunisia objects when music is used to enliven Security Council's technical meetings on sanctions against Iraq (S)

2001-06-12.title: Charles Sews Up Fashion, De la Renta Sows His Garden : War of Roses, And Parterres
2001-06-12.abstract: WADDESDON MANOR, England—  It is a battle royal. Hoes, rakes and spades at dawn. On one side of the garden fence is Prince Charles, who famously talks to his plants, has a green thumb, nurtures wild flowers and has just shown off an Islamic-style patio at England's Chelsea Flower Show.

2001-06-12.title: Israeli-Palestinian Negotiations Break Off After 4 Hours
2001-06-12.abstract: Israeli and Palestinian security officials meet for four hours, but reportedly fail to reach agreement on American-initiated proposal that would extend fragile and partial 10-day-old cease-fire; closed session was mediated by CIA director, George J Tenet; photo (M)

2001-06-13.title: Malaysia's 'Great Builder' Faces Growing Dissent
2001-06-13.abstract: PUTRAJAYA, Malaysia—  As he nears the anniversary of two decades in power, Mahathir bin Mohamad spends most of his time in a newly built, green-domed office overlooking the beginnings of a grand city being built from scratch.

2001-06-13.title: World Briefing | Europe: Russia: An Honor And A Barb For Yeltsin
2001-06-13.abstract: Pres Vladimir V Putin decorates his predecessor, Boris N Yeltsin, with new medal marking his service to nation; ceremony marks Russia Day, observance of Russia's 1990 declaration that it would pursue democracy within Soviet Union; photo (S)

2001-06-13.title: World Briefing | World: A Pledge On Child Labor
2001-06-13.abstract: Internatinoal Labor Organization reports that El Salvador, Nepal and Tanzania have pledge to end worst forms of child labor within 10 years (S)

2001-06-13.title: Suharto Collapses; Taken to Hospital
2001-06-13.abstract: Former Pres Suharto of Indonesia is hospitalized in Jakarta after collapsing at home (S)

2001-06-13.title: World Briefing | Asia: Myanmar: Accusations Of Forced Labor
2001-06-13.abstract: Two leading human rights groups contend Myanmar's military government continues to employ forced labor in practice that was recently condemned by ILO (S)

2001-06-14.title: A Top Swiss Official Resigns In Money-Laundering Dispute
2001-06-14.abstract: Niklaus Huber abruptly resigns as head of Swiss government office fighting financial crime in wake of complaints in Parliament that Swiss officials have underestimated scope of money laundering and have not taken sufficient steps to combat it; is second top official to resign in less than year in dispute over strategy for dealing with money laundering (M)

2001-06-14.title: World Briefing | Americas: Guatemala: Ex-Dictator Faces Inquiry
2001-06-14.abstract: Guatemalan court orders prosecutors to investigate Efrain Rios Montt, former dictator and current speaker of Congress, on charges of masterminding massacres of tens of thousands of people during country's civil war (S)

2001-06-14.title: Some Russians Are Alarmed At Tighter Grip Under Putin
2001-06-14.abstract: Russian intellectuals and democracy advocates fear relative freedom from state surveillance and restriction relished in last decade may be drawing to close; see resurgence in government obsession with internal security and threats from abroad that has set off limited but growing crackdown on supposed challenges to state; are unsettled by Pres Vladimir V Putin's failure to restrain or speak out against signs of crackdown; photo (M)

2001-06-14.title: World Briefing | World: Saving Sturgeon, And Caviar
2001-06-14.abstract: Russia, Kazakhstan, Turkmenistan, Azerbaijan--former Soviet republics--agree to protect dwindling population of caviar-producing sturgeon; photo (S)

2001-06-14.title: Shanghai Journal; Political Power Grows From the Point of His Pen
2001-06-14.abstract: Interview with Peng Ruigao, Shanghai government propaganda bureau worker, one of thousands of Communist Party functionaries who write slogans party peppers people with daily; party is ever present in China with billboards and banners urging citizens to follow its plan; public display of verses, fixture since ancient times, shifted to propaganda slogans after Qing dynasty fell in 1911; photo (M)

2001-06-15.title: Police Killings of Israeli Arabs Being Questioned by Inquiry
2001-06-15.abstract: Formal inquiry this month into police killing of 13 people in violent protests by Israeli Arabs in October raises questions about several of shootings, particularly whether police needed to fire live ammunition; it has also served as platform to air what Israeli Arabs say is discrimination that has gone on for decades; photo (M)

2001-06-15.title: World Briefing | Europe: Vatican: Fighting The Priest Drain
2001-06-15.abstract: Vatican, warning that too many third-world priests trained in Europe are staying there for lifestyle reasons, issues pamphlet clarifying how priests and missionaries should be deployed to help their own people at home (S)

2001-06-15.title: China Said to Sharply Reduce Emissions of Carbon Dioxide
2001-06-15.abstract: Chinese and Western energy experts say that China has already achieved dramatic slowing in its emissions of carbon dioxide in last decade; say that record of progress has pushed further into horizon the day that China will surpass United States as lead culprit in atmospheric pollution, and it is something that Pres Bush seems to have overlooked in his harsh appraisal of runaway Chinese emissions; specter of Cnina's runaway pollutants has been cited by Bush as a major reason for describing 1997 Kyoto agreement to protect environment as 'fatally flawed'; Chinese officials insist that their country will do its fair share to combat a serious global threat (M)

2001-06-15.title: World Briefing | Asia: India: Sonia Gandhi To Visit U.S.
2001-06-15.abstract: Sonia Gandhi, president of Congress Party and widowed heir to Nehru-Gandhi dynasty, will make her first trip to United States as leader of opposition to Indian government; will speak at Asia Society in New York; photo (S)

2001-06-15.title: Deep U.S.-Europe Split Casts Long Shadow on Bush Tour
2001-06-15.abstract: Pres Bush and European leaders express sharp differences and conflicting intentions today about global warming at a summit meeting of European Union and United States; it is Bush's first appearance at a European Union meeting, and his first introduction to several of leaders, but it also second day in row that he finds himself at odds with European officials; photo of Bush with Finnish Pres Tarja Halonen (M)

2001-06-16.title: Chinese Test New Weapon From West: Lawsuits
2001-06-16.abstract: Young Chinese are testing weapon borrowed from West: lawsuits; number of lawsuits in Chinese courts has grown exponentially, including suits for defective products, wrongful injury, poor service and wrongful dismissal; as legal awareness improves and legal system develops, more and more people are going to court to defend their rights; Chinese have high hopes about power of lawsuits to remedy injustice; cases reflect growing concern about rights among Chinese themselves, even as their government faces criticism from abroad of its poor rights record; photo of two workers who have sued their former employer (M)

2001-06-16.title: PRESIDENT URGING EXPANSION OF NATO TO RUSSIA'S BORDER
2001-06-16.abstract: Pres Bush, speaking in Warsaw, urges broad, steady expansion of NATO into countries of Eastern Europe that are not now in alliance; seeks to reassure Russian government that this is not confrontational strategy, saying neither United States nor Europe are enemies of Russia and that Russia is indeed part of Europe; says historic pacts that assigned European countries to different superpowers' spheres of influence are moribund; this is Bush's most detailed and sweeping remarks yet about future of relationship between United States and Europe (M)

2001-06-16.title: Demonstrators Clash Sharply With Police In Sweden
2001-06-16.abstract: Swedish police brace for fresh protests in Goteborg, site of summit meeting of European Union leaders; earlier riots leave dozens injured, hundreds arrested and center of city looking like war zone; hundreds of protesters set fires, smash shop windows and charge police officers in riot gear; thousands of demonstrators who poured into city in last two days are protesting array of issues; organized groups are looking to draw attention to everything from energy policies to what they believe are dangers of globalization; photo (M)

2001-06-16.title: World Briefing | Europe: Germany: First Slave Labor Payments
2001-06-16.abstract: German officials announce first payments from $4.4 billion fund of German Foundation to compensate former slaves and forced laborers for Nazis; says it transferred $44 million to United States-based Jewish Claims Conference, $25 million to Poland, and $24.5 million to Czech Republic (S)

2001-06-16.title: World Briefing | Europe: Russia: Public Fight Over Private Property
2001-06-16.abstract: Fistfight breaks out in Russian Parliament after government official presents draft bill on private ownership of land; Communist lawmakers try to block official from making way to podium but bill is approved on first of three required readings; photo (S)

2001-06-17.title: Annan Urges Mideast to Make Peace Move
2001-06-17.abstract: UN Sec Gen Kofi Annan urges Israelis and Palestinians to seize moment and move toward resumption of peace talks; comments after meeting with Yasir Arafat in Ramallah, hours before violence breaks out anew (S)

2001-06-17.title: PUTIN URGES BUSH NOT TO ACT ALONE ON MISSILE SHIELD
2001-06-17.abstract: Pres Putin, in first meeting with Pres Bush, warns that developing missile defense shield without Russian consent would seriously strain relations bewteen two countries; surprisingly gentle warning is tucked into amicable news conference at castle in Slovenia; photos; upbeat statements also suggest at least some cooperation on divisive security issues, although Putin makes clear his opposition to Bush's ambitious military plan, calling 1972 ABM treaty 'cornerstone' of modern international security; adds hope for 'constructive dialogue'; Bush, all smiles and laughs, says he 'looked the man in the eye' and got 'sense of his soul' (M)

2001-06-17.title: First Meeting Between Bush and Putin
2001-06-17.abstract: Following are the statements and answers yesterday by President Bush and President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia after their meeting, as transcribed by the Federal News Service, Inc. Mr. Putin's statements were made through an interpreter.

2001-06-17.title: Two Leaders: Retelling a 'Productive' Exchange
2001-06-17.abstract: Following are excerpts from the statements and answers yesterday by President Bush and President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia after their meeting, as transcribed by the Federal News Service, Inc. Mr. Putin's statements were made through an interpreter.

2001-06-17.title: South Korea Nudges North Toward New Talks
2001-06-17.abstract: President Kim Dae Jung of South Korea got the kind of endorsements he wanted for an inter-Korean peace effort that is at risk of stagnating.

2001-06-18.title: Putin Urges Global Pressure To Disarm Rebels in Kosovo
2001-06-18.abstract: Russia and Yugoslavia say Kosovo is main cause of instability in Balkans and urges world leaders to ensure that Albanian 'terrorists' in Kosovo and region are completely disarmed; Russian Pres Vladimir V Putin, first Russian president to visit Yugoslavia in over decade, warns Macedonia runs risk of becoming another Kosovo if Western pressure pushes Macedonian leadership to give in to demands of rebels (M)

2001-06-18.title: U.N. Sanctions Didn't Stop Iraq From Buying Weapons
2001-06-18.abstract: Report by American arms control experts, Gary Milhollin and Kelly Motz, who combed through unpublished reports by disbanded arms inspection commission, say they found evidence that Iraq continued to buy prohibited weapons or parts long after UN sanctions were imposed in 1990; say many of purchases appear to have been made in Central and Eastern Europe; found documents concerning illegal sales or potential sales by companies in Ukraine, Belarus and Romania; says Pres Saddam Hussein's procurement network is alive and well and has been working steadily despite sanctions; report comes as UN Security Council debates new oil-for-food program for Iraq that would lift most restrictions on sales of civilian goods (M)

2001-06-18.title: Early Results Show Ex-King Leads Vote In Bulgaria
2001-06-18.abstract: National Movement for Simeon II, party of Bulgaria's former king, leads in vote in Bulgarian elections, even though it appeared to fall short of declared goal of clear majority, dealing heavy blow to political forces that have governed Bulgaria since collapse of Communism in 1989; photo (M)

2001-06-18.title: Cordial Rivals: How Bush And Putin Became Friends
2001-06-18.abstract: Official who helped plan Pres Bush's European trip says warm rapport shown between him and Russian Pres Vladimir Putin represents significant achievement for US; critics on Capitol Hill say Bush was too willing to praise Putin as trustworthy after meeting for only first time; Democrats who control Senate are relieved that Bush's visit did not go badly, but some say he went too far in cozying up to Putin; photo (M)

2001-06-18.title: Lake Titicaca Journal; On a Lily Pad, Life Is Lush, but Watch Your Step
2001-06-18.abstract: Article on Uro Indians of Peru, who live on 45 man-made islands built from reeds on Lake Titicaca; maps; photos (M)

2001-06-19.title: North Korea Rebuffs U.S. on Troop Talks
2001-06-19.abstract: North Korea, in first official reaction to American proposals to resume talks, dismisses Bush administration request that issue of conventional forces be included along with questions of nuclear and ballistic missile control; accuses Pres Bush of trying to unilaterally set agenda for talks; says US must remove its 37,000 troops from South Korea before any discussions of North Korean troop deployments are possible (M)

2001-06-19.title: Afghan Rulers To Let Women Carry Out A U.N. Survey
2001-06-19.abstract: Taliban government of Afghanistan will allow World Food Program to employ local women to survey food needs of most vulnerable households in country, which is suffering effects of drought and famine, after yearlong standoff; under agreement, agency will be allowed to select, hire and train 30 women, initially, from list of potential employees drawn up by Ministry of Health (M)

2001-06-19.title: Sharon Faces New Pressure As 2 Drivers Are Killed
2001-06-19.abstract: Two Israeli drivers are killed in shootings in West Bank; attacks follow reports of mortar firings and discovery of bombs in Haifa, city little touched by violence of last eight months; Prime Min Ariel Sharon rejects demands by Jewish settlers that he call off Israel's tentative truce with Palestinians and respond to drive-by attacks with military force (M)

2001-06-19.title: World Briefing | Europe: Britain: Second Hat In Tory Ring
2001-06-19.abstract: Iain Duncan Smith becomes candidate to succeed British Conservative Party leader William Hague; represents party's right wing and has Lady Thatcher's backing; seeks to block Michael Portillo (S)

2001-06-19.title: Yemen Arrests Suspects in Plot on U.S. Agents for Bomb Case
2001-06-19.abstract: Yemen reports arrest of group of suspects in connection with possible terrorist plot against FBI and US Navy personnel investigating October 2000 bombing of US destroyer Cole in port of Aden; say suspects may have been planning attack against US Embassy in capital of Sana (S)

2001-06-20.title: Bush to Meet With Sharon, Keeping Arafat at Arm's Length
2001-06-20.abstract: Prime Minr Ariel Sharon will meet with Pres Bush at White House for second time since Sharon's election in Israel; Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat, by contrast, has not been invited to Washington by Bush administration, and officials make clear they have no plans to do so in near future (S)

2001-06-20.title: Troubles Tarnish a Once-Shining African Airline
2001-06-20.abstract: Article on woes of Air Afrique, founded in 1961 and run by 11 former French colonies as symbol of their independence; creditors are now threatening to seize six remaining planes, and airline's fate lies with World Bank; airline's symbolism has always been more potent than reality, which is that it was run by French crews and executives; descent has been precipitous since Africans took over top posts in early 1980's; photos (M)

2001-06-20.title: World Briefing | Middle East: Israel: Disco Toll Rises To 21
2001-06-20.abstract: 15-year-old girl dies of wounds in suicide bombing of Tel Aviv disco on June 1, bringing death toll to 21 (S)

2001-06-20.title: Ex-Diplomat To Lead Group In AIDS Battle
2001-06-20.abstract: Richard C Holbrooke, former UN ambassador who is now vice chairman of Perseus LLC, is also serving as unpaid president of Global Business Council on HIV and AIDS, group formed in London in 1997; he discusses efforts to press businesses to educate employees on AIDS prevention, interview (S)

2001-06-20.title: Specter of a Rearmed Japan Stirs Its Wartime Generation
2001-06-20.abstract: Japan is on brink of its first real passionate public debate on long-taboo subject of its twentieth-century history of conquest and defeat; rare national conversation is provoked by Prime Min Junichiro Koizumi's proposal to break with decades of official pacifism and revise US-written postwar constitution that bars Japan from raising an army; debate is largely confined to those alive during war, like army veteran Shigeru Mizuki, since younger Japanese display only sketchy knowledge of imperial conquest; Japanese who lived through era offer personal memories of its immense costs, and warn against vogue for revisionism; interviews; photos; they also have deeply mixed feelings about Koizumi's intention of visiting Shinto shrine to war dead; that plan enrages China and Korea, with their histories of brutal occupation; millions honored at shrine include wartime leader Tojo and other war criminals (M)

2001-06-21.title: Powell Going To Mideast As Cease-Fire Seems in Peril
2001-06-21.abstract: White House says Secretary of State Colin L Powell will travel to Middle East next week to help shore up teetering cease-fire and try to push Israelis and Palestinians on path toward political talks; timing of his trip comes as surprise and underlines Bush administration's fear that progress of last week is in severe danger of falling apart; administration finds itself more and more involved in day-to-day management of Israeli-Palestinian conflict, approach for which administration officials criticized Pres Clinton and vowed early in their tenure that they would not copy (M)

2001-06-21.title: NATO IS PLANNING MACEDONIA FORCE
2001-06-21.abstract: NATO will send force of up to 3,000 troops to Macedonia to disarm ethnic Albanian reels if political settlement is reached to end insurgency there; mission would probably be led by British and draw predominantly on forces from European nations; United States is likely to provide transport, intelligence and other forms of support, but not troops, in keeping with Bush administration's skeptical approach toward peacekeeping; NATO force was requested by Pres Boris Trajkovski of Macedonia; peace talks are stalemated and shaky cease-fire declared by his government is to expire on June 25; International Crisis Group, research organization, warns that situation is perilous and getting more dangerous every day, raising prospect of all-out civil war; NATO secretary general Lord Robertson agrees that Macedonia is 'close to civil war' (M)

2001-06-21.title: 20-Year Sentence for New Yorker After 2nd Terrorism Trial in Peru
2001-06-21.abstract: Lori Berenson, 31-year-old New Yorker held for more than five years in Peruvian prisons on terrorism charges, is convicted by civilian court of collaborating in foiled terrorist plot to take entire Peruvian Congress hostage, and sentenced to 20 years in prison; three-judge tribunal in Lima rules that she rented house in Lima with co-conspirator to provide base for Marxist terrorist organization, Tupac Amaru Revolutionary Movement (MRTA), bought computer and communications gear for group, and used press credentials as cover to scrutinize halls of Congress and facilitate eventual attack; Berenson claims she is innocent of charges, but she again refuses to condemn MRTA; her parents and lawyer say they will appeal to Peruvian Supreme Court and international human rights tribunals; photo (M)

2001-06-21.title: Pakistan's Military Ruler Declares Himself President
2001-06-21.abstract: Gen Pervez Musharraf appoints himself president and formal head of state of Pakistan, after toppling civilian government 20 months ago and assuming unbridled power under misleadingly modest title of chief executive; he renews assurances that he will return country to civilian rule after elections in Oct 2002, date that was set by Pakistan's Supreme Court when it endorsed his bloodless military takeover but stipulated three-year deadline for restoring democracy; Musharraf's assumption of title held by civilian president, Rafiq Tara, appears to set stage for form of 'guided democracy' that would leave him with final authority once elected government is installed; in Washington, State Dept spokesman Richard A Boucher says Musharraf's action 'severely undermines' Pakistan's constitutional order; photo (M)

2001-06-21.title: Varadero Journal; Just Watch the Golfers Frolic in Proletarian Cuba
2001-06-21.abstract: Cuba's only 18-hole golf course is in Varadero, where Du Pont family once owned beachfront mansion; it is such a popular tourist draw that Cuban officials eager to attract duffers and their dollars are planning to build as many as 5 to 10 more courses; photo; map (M)

2001-06-22.title: World Briefing | Asia: Myanmar: Five Opposition Lawmakers Freed
2001-06-22.abstract: Myanmar military regime releases five opposition lawmakers detained since 1998 (S)

2001-06-22.title: Killers of British Toddler to Be Freed
2001-06-22.abstract: LONDON, June 22 — The government said today that two British teenagers who kidnapped and killed a toddler when they were 10 have cleared their final hurdle for parole and are to be released imminently with newly constructed identities.

2001-06-22.title: World Briefing | Middle East: Gaza Strip: Cease-Fire Holds
2001-06-22.abstract: Palestinians in Gaza fire large-caliber mortar shell at border crossing into Israel and fire antitank grenades at Israeli troops near Egyptian border (S)

2001-06-22.title: 2nd Night of Protestant-Catholic Rioting in Belfast
2001-06-22.abstract: Clashes between Roman Catholic and Protestant residents of Belfast, Northern Ireland, continue in second consecutive night of rioting; photo (S)

2001-06-22.title: World Briefing | Asia: East Timor: 250 New Soldiers
2001-06-22.abstract: First group of 250 soldiers graduate from training academy as East Timor prepares for independence (S)

2001-06-23.title: Macedonia Launches an Offensive Against Albanian Rebels
2001-06-23.abstract: Macedonian forces launch heavy offensive against ethnic Albanian rebel positions north of Skopje, breaking tentative cease-fire and upsetting diplomatic peace efforts; rebels apparently repel main assault on Aracinovo; Lord Robertson, Nato secretary general, scores assault; diplomats call drive effort to gain upper hand in intraparty talks (M)

2001-06-23.title: Turkish Court Bans Religious Party, Main Opposition Force
2001-06-23.abstract: Turkey's highest court bans religious-oriented Virtue Party, holding main opposition party violated law barring religious activities that could undermine secular government; stops short of expelling large numbers of party from Parliament, where party controls 102 of 550 seats; expells two members from Parliament and bans five others from politics for five years; Prime Min Bulent Ecevit rules out early elections, citing need to focus on economic woes; photo (M)

2001-06-23.title: World Briefing | Europe: Switzerland: A Vote For The U.N.
2001-06-23.abstract: Swiss Parliament votes overwhelmingly in favor of joining United Nations, bolstering government's plan to put issue to referendum in 2002 (S)

2001-06-23.title: World Briefing | Asia: Cambodia: 5 Sentenced To Life For Terrorism

2001-06-24.title: In Ukraine, Pope Tries to Heal Rift With Orthodox Church
2001-06-24.abstract: Pope John Paul II, setting aside objections from Orthodox church that broke with Rome millennium ago, makes his first visit to Ukraine and tries to placate his unwilling hosts; says he did not come to Ukraine with intention of proselytizing, airport ceremony, Kiev; urges both churches to 'asks forgiveness' of each other and not to let past errors hinder 'mutual knowledge'; Metropolitan Vladimir, who heads largest Ukrainian Orthodox church, manages to be out of town to avoid chance of meeting Pope; pope's visit is welcome to Pres Leonid Kuchma, whose scandal-ridden administration badly needs a lift; photo (M)

2001-06-24.title: Struggling to Carve Out Common Ground, U.N. Tackles AIDS
2001-06-24.abstract: United Nations seeks to forge consensus for action on tackling AIDS, now estimated to be carried by 36 million people worldwide; starting June 25, 3,000 diplomats, government officials, health experts and members of advocacy groups will gather for three days in General Assembly to discuss how to cope with medical catastrophe that has accumulated political baggage since virus was identified two decades ago (M)

2001-06-24.title: Ulster Power Sharing Is Threatened if I.R.A. Stays Armed
2001-06-24.abstract: David Trimble says he holds little hope of any imminent disarmament moves by Irish Republican Army; as a consequence, he would carry out his threat to force a halt to power-sharing Northern Ireland Assembly next week; tells meeting of his Ulster Unionist party that he will keep his pledge that unless IRA acts to disarm by July 1, he will resign his first minister position (M)

2001-06-24.title: Dozens Die as Quake Strikes Southern Peru
2001-06-24.abstract: A powerful earthquake shook southern Peru, killing at least 40 people, injuring hundreds of others and sending dozens of homes, churches and buildings tumbling to the ground.

2001-06-24.title: Japan's New Superstar Redefines Its Politics
2001-06-24.abstract: Japan's new Prime Min, Junichiro Koizumi, has emerged as most popular prime minister Japan has ever seen, even though unemployment and economic growth figures have been as dismal as ever; turnaround is seen remarkable, as Koizumi was close ally of departing prime min, least popular in recent history; his long-ruling Liberal Democratic Party is now expected to do well in local Tokyo elections for first time in years; ironically, many of prime minister's harshest critics are old-style bosses within his own party, but they dare not raise their voices in public while Koizumi vogue continues (M)

2001-06-25.title: Harsh Chinese Realities Feed Market in Women
2001-06-25.abstract: Article on trafficking in women in China, where tens or hundreds of thousands of poor women are sold on black market each year; trade thrives in rural areas, where there are nearly 120 boys for every 100 girls but where it is considered culturally and economically essential that 100 percent of men find brides and produce heirs; for every 100 rural men who marry, 20 others mustr resort to extraordinary measures to find brides, like buying kidnapped women; government is waging harsh campaign against practice which, though significantly dampened, still thrives in rural areas; photo (M)

2001-06-25.title: North and South Korean Vessels Clash at Sea
2001-06-25.abstract: North Korean fishermen fire flares and brandished sticks and knives at two approaching South Korean patrol boats in Yellow Sea on June 24 before fleeing in boat after South Koreans fire nine warning shots (S)

2001-06-25.title: OBITUARY : Francois Lesure, Musicologist
2001-06-25.abstract: PARIS — Francois Lesure, 78, biographer of the composer Claude Debussy and former director of the music department of the French Bibliotheque Nationale, died Thursday of a heart attack at his home here.

2001-06-25.title: Chile's Effort to Try Pinochet Is Running Out of Steam
2001-06-25.abstract: Legal experts and human rights activists say prosecution of former Chilean dictator Augusto Pinochet, on charges of covering up murders and disappearances of scores of dissidents in 1973, is moving toward slow procedural death; his lawyers have changed legal tack in recent months by arguing that 85-year-old client is mentally unfit to stand trial; appeal has gained support among political establishment and appears likely to be accepted in court (M)

2001-06-25.title: Milosevic Fights Transfer To Hague Trial
2001-06-25.abstract: Lawyers for former Yugoslav Pres Slobodan Milosevic will challenge new government decree that commits authorities to hand over indicted war criminals like Milosevic to international tribunal in The Hague (S)

2001-06-26.title: World Briefing | Asia: Nepal: Premier Urged To Quit
2001-06-26.abstract: Nepal's Parliament convenes for first time since shooting deaths of King Birendra, Queen Aishwariya and other members of royal family three weeks ago; Communist-led opposition demands resignation of Prime Min Girija Prasad Koirala's government on ground that Koirala 'failed to protect the lives' of royal family (S)

2001-06-26.title: Belgrade Begins Process of Sending Milosevic to The Hague
2001-06-26.abstract: Yugoslav government takes first legal steps to hand over former President Slobodan Milosevic to international war crimes tribunal in The Hague, raising expectations that he might be transferred within week before international conference that could bring aid to Serbia; forwards request from tribunal for Milosevic's transfer to Belgrade district court; ignores legal challenge by Milosevic's lawyers calling decree unconstitutional and asking that it be suspended (M)

2001-06-26.title: World Briefing | Europe: Italy: Illegal Immigrants Caught
2001-06-26.abstract: Fishing boat carrying 199 illegal immigrants from Bangladesh, Afghanistan and other countries is intercepted by coast guard off southern Italy; in separate case, 24 illegal immigrants, reportedly Turkish Kurds, are picked up near highway in Calabria (S)

2001-06-26.title: Former Spy Chief of Peru Is Flown to Lima to Face Charges
2001-06-26.abstract: Vladimiro Montesinos, former Peruvian spy chief who evaded international manhunt for eight months before arrest in Venezuela on June 23, is flown back to Peru; faces possible life sentence if convicted on wide range of charges; is being interrogated by six investigative judges in underground cell in courthouse in downtown Lima; Peruvian law enforcement officials hope he will lead them to many corrupt officials still in Peru's bureaucracy; photo (M)

2001-06-27.title: New Dress Code Takes Shape
2001-06-27.abstract: LONDON—  Dressing down is dead. Welcome to the third way of business attire. With dot-boom turned to dot-bust, the deregulated business uniform of chinos and sports shirts has lost its appeal, and men are frantically looking for a new set of rules to help them dress appropriately for their business dealings.

2001-06-27.title: World Briefing | Americas: Argentina: Ex-President Still Silent
2001-06-27.abstract: Investigative judge calls Carlos Saul Menem, former Argentine president, to court for second time to answer accusations that he led conspiracy to sell arms illegally to Croatia and Ecuador (S)

2001-06-27.title: 4 Youths Held in London in Killing of Boy, 10
2001-06-27.abstract: British police bring murder charges against four teenagers in killing of 10-year-old immigrant from Nigeria; stabbing death of Damilola Taylor on stairwell of London housing project caused revulsion because of evidence that passers-by left boy to bleed to death from leg wound; his photo (M)

2001-06-27.title: World Briefing | Americas: Chile: Pinochet Hearing Delayed
2001-06-27.abstract: Appeals court in Chile delays hearing on whether to suspend human rights case against Gen Augusto Pinochet after one of three judges fails to show up (S)

2001-06-27.title: Gucci Strikes The Right Mix
2001-06-27.abstract: MILAN—  Female fashion's phobias and fears are summed up in the query: "Does my butt look big in this?"

2001-06-28.title: World Briefing | Americas: Peru: Quake Toll Passes 100
2001-06-28.abstract: Death toll from powerful earthquake in southern Peru is increased to 102, with 53 people still missing; Pres Valentin Paniagua declares state of emergency (S)

2001-06-28.title: French Judges Seek to Question Chirac, His Wife and Daughter
2001-06-28.abstract: French judges want to question Pres Jacques Chirac, his wife and his daughter about $300,000 in cash that was used to buy airplane tickets while Chirac was mayor of Paris; judges suspect Chirac used illegal money to finance trips for himself and his family; Chirac, who is expected to seek re-electon next year, is also subject of other inquiries, including his role in huge illicit fund-raising scandal that has already prompted some in Parliament to pursue impeachment proceedings (M)

2001-06-28.title: U.N. REDEFINES AIDS AS POLITICAL ISSUE AND PERIL TO POOR
2001-06-28.abstract: General Assembly concludes three-day special session with ambitious declaration outlining how world should proceed in its fight against AIDS; makes plea to nations and private industry to provide billions of dollars needed to help pay for mission; so-called Declaration of Commitment is extraordinary in its language and its tact; it views AIDS not only as medical problem but also as political, human rights and economic threat; it also addresses issues like role that exploitation of poor women and discrimination plays in spreading HIV; Dr Peter Piot, executive director of Unaids, UN agency coordinating fight against AIDS, says key goal is 25 percent reduction of HIV infection among young men and women in most affected countries by 2005; Piot and Secretary General Kofi Annan realize that many of document's elements will be hard sell in nations where rural women enjoy few rights, where gays are stigmatized and where sexual education is generally eschewed (M)

2001-06-28.title: World Briefing | Asia: Philippines: Ex-President In Court
2001-06-28.abstract: Joseph Estrada, former Philippine president, refuses to enter plea to perjury charge stemming from corruption scandal; court enters plea of not guilty on his behalf; photo (S)

2001-06-28.title: French Court Approves Extradition of American Fugitive
2001-06-28.abstract: RENNES, France, June 28 — A French court today agreed to extradite James Charles Kopp, an American anti-abortion activist accused in the sniper killing of an upstate New York doctor.

2001-06-29.title: Rome Journal; Colorful Characters Lurk Around Monuments
2001-06-29.abstract: Italy's new and more conservative Silvio Berlusconi administration is contesting, reviling and reconsidering variety of artistic restoration projects planned by previous government, most especially exemplified by debate over Ara Pacis, Augustan 'altar of peace' dedicated in AD 9; Vittorio Sgarbi, celebrity art critic and newly appointed under secretary of culture, is noisily battling vast architectural project in Rome by Richard Meier, American architect; construction has already begun on starkly modern building and museum Meier designed to replace it; Sgarbi says he will 'negotiate' with Meier to improve project; clash over Ara Pacis captures complexity of Italian culture and politics; photo (L)

2001-06-29.title: Outpouring of Loyalty as Milosevic Departs
2001-06-29.abstract: Transfer of Slobodan Milosevic brings joy and relief to some in Belgrade, particularly the young, angers others in its speed and stealth, and leaves many more ambivalent for their deep distrust of Hague tribunal they feel is biased against Serbs (M)

2001-06-29.title: Powell Backing Plan to Monitor Mideast Truce
2001-06-29.abstract: Secretary of State Colin L Powell, in Israel, says observers would be needed to monitor cease-fire between Israelis and Palestinians; announces seven-week timetable for two sides to end hostilities and move toward political talks; timetable calls on both sides to prevent all clashes for seven days, then enter into six-week 'cooling off' period; plan combines Israeli Prime Min Ariel Sharon's insistence on week without violence before any further measures are agreed to, with notion that two sides would need several weeks to retreat from bitter hostility and bloodshed of past nine months; Powell says two sides must decide together how to measure reduction of violence; aides to Powell say he got renewed commitment from Yasir Arafat to order his commanders to stop attacks on Israelis, and from Sharon he got pledge to freeze settlement activity; photo (M)

2001-06-29.title: From the U.N.'s Statement on AIDS: 'Prevention Must Be the Mainstay'
2001-06-29.abstract: Excerpts from Declaration of Commitment on HIV/AIDS adopted by United Nations General Assembly (L)

2001-06-30.title: Iran-Contra Figure Back at White House
2001-06-30.abstract: Pres Bush names Elliott Abrams, prominent figure in Iran-contra scandal of 1980's, to national security council as director of its office for democracy, human rights and international operations (S)

2001-06-30.title: Diplomacy by BMW, With the Top Up
2001-06-30.abstract: King Abdullah of Jordan greets Sec of State Colin L Powell in Amman and invites him to get behind the wheel of his custom-made two-seater BMW convertible; photo of Powell and Abdullah before Powell's flight to Paris (S)

2001-06-30.title: World Briefing | Asia: Japan: Census Shows Gray
World Briefing | Europe: France: Fetus Not A Person, Court Says
2001-06-30.abstract: French appeals court rules that drunken driver cannot be charged with manslaughter in death of a fetus; ruling stems from incident six years ago when pregnant woman was hit by drunken driver and her baby was still-born four days later (S)

2001-06-30.title: Premier of Yugoslavia Resigns in Protest
2001-06-30.abstract: Handing over of Slobodan Milosevic to United Nations war crimes tribunal prompts Yugoslav Prime Minister Zoran Zizic to resign, bringing down Yugoslav government and opening first cracks in alliance of democratic reformers who removed Milosevic from power eight months ago; several thousand Milosevic supporters rally in front of Yugoslav Parliament (M)
